stories.title: NYC attacker yelled 'Allahu Akbar' as he randomly stabbed two men near Central Park, according to victim, witnesses: commish
stories.summary: A 51-year-old man, Raul Morales, was arrested Thursday after yelling "Allahu Akbar" while stabbing two men in separate attacks near Central Park in Manhattan. Both victims, aged 57 and 50, were hospitalized with nonfatal injuries and are expected to survive. Police are investigating whether hate or mental health factors motivated the assaults.

stories.title: The Game Recalls Early Encounters With Nipsey Hussle During VERZUZ With YG
stories.summary: During a VERZUZ clash with YG on June 23, The Game honored late rapper Nipsey Hussle by recalling their early friendship and performing their 2008 collaboration "They Roll." Travis Barker joined The Game for a surprise drum performance of "Dope Boys" to kick off the event. The livestream is available on Apple Music and YouTube.

stories.title: YG and The Game's 'VERZUZ': Travis Barker Pops Out for Opening Cut "Dope Boys"
stories.summary: Travis Barker joined The Game on drums for the opening of his VERZUZ battle with YG on July 23, delivering a high-energy performance of “Dope Boys.” The surprise appearance thrilled fans during the livestream on Apple Music and YouTube. The event continues with the rappers trading verses live.

stories.title: Measles surges dramatically in California among children and young adults
stories.summary: Measles cases in California have more than doubled this year compared to last year, with 52 confirmed cases in the first 6½ months of 2026, a pace that could lead to the highest number of cases since 2015. The majority of cases involve children and young adults, with 27 cases among those aged 5 to 19 and 15 cases among children under 5 years old. If this trend continues, the state may see its highest measles case total since a large outbreak at Disneyland in 2015.

stories.title: ‘Sia: Nostalgic for the Present’ Review: A Concert Performance Film from 2016, but It Channels Sia’s Mystery and Power
stories.summary: Sia’s 2016 concert film Nostalgic for the Present showcases her iconic anthems like "Unstoppable" and "Titanium" through a faceless performance that amplifies their themes of strength. The film’s abstract visuals turn her into a vessel for a broader message of feminine empowerment. It remains a striking testament to her enduring influence.

stories.title: HHS Changes Two Vaccine Committee Charters
stories.summary: US Health and Human Services has altered the charters of two vaccine-related committees, the Advisory Commission on Childhood Vaccines and the National Vaccine Advisory Committee, with changes including the removal of a designated federal officer from the first committee and the addition of a focus on non-immunization strategies for the second. The changes are set to take effect immediately, with the committees advising on policies for the National Vaccine Injury Compensation Program and national vaccine strategy respectively. The implications of these changes are unclear, but experts have expressed concern over the potential impact on vaccine policy.

stories.title: Burnham to spend first day working from northern branch of No 10
stories.summary: Andy Burnham will hold his first meeting at the new No 10 North office in Manchester today, marking a push to decentralise government power from London. Up to 300 civil servants will relocate to the site by the end of next year as part of broader devolution plans. The prime minister will chair a National Economic Council meeting to prioritise regional investment and local growth.

stories.title: US imposes tariffs on dozens of trade partners over 'forced labour' imports
stories.summary: The US is imposing new tariffs of 10% to 12.5% on imports from 60 trading partners, including the UK, China, the EU, Canada, Japan and India, over forced labour concerns. The duties take effect Friday as a temporary 10% tax expires. The move escalates trade tensions amid ongoing global disputes.

stories.title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4954–4960: Celebrating Our Rover Engineers Past and Present
stories.summary: NASA’s Curiosity rover completed its latest science campaign on Mars this week, analyzing five rock targets with its APXS instrument and MAHLI imager. Engineers overcame dusty, uneven terrain to safely deploy the robotic arm despite challenging conditions. The team now plans to continue driving toward a sulfate-rich area to study Mars’ ancient climate history.

stories.title: Indonesian babies were trafficked to Singapore. What happens to them now?
stories.summary: Indonesian babies trafficked to Singapore face an uncertain future after a court convicted 19 people for selling at least 34 infants, with 12 sent to Singapore. The verdict leaves adoptive parents in limbo, awaiting decisions on whether the children will remain or return to Indonesia. Authorities in both countries are now under pressure to resolve the children’s status promptly.

stories.title: Trump Administration to Impose New Tariffs of Around 10% on Over 80 Nations
stories.summary: The administration announced new duties tied to countries’ imports of goods made with forced labor, replacing a global 10 percent tariff set to expire at midnight.

stories.title: Heatwave anxiety can make you feel 'incredibly helpless' - here's how to manage it
stories.summary: A record June heatwave in England, peaking above 30C in some homes, left parents like Tavia Watts feeling "incredibly helpless" as anxiety surged. Watts, a stay-at-home mum in Cheltenham, shared her fears online, sparking thousands of reactions as UK climate anxiety affects an estimated 4.6% of people. Experts warn the mental toll of extreme heat will likely grow as temperatures rise.

stories.title: China's Moonshot AI stole from Anthropic, Trump tech adviser says
stories.summary: China’s Moonshot AI allegedly stole advanced AI capabilities from US firm Anthropic by using a technique called distillation to extract answers from stronger models. A White House adviser said Moonshot accessed restricted Nvidia servers to train its K3 model, which was unveiled last week and is seen as a major step for Chinese AI. The US restricted Nvidia’s top chips in 2022 over security concerns.

stories.title: US health panel recommends easing restrictions on controversial peptides
stories.summary: A federal health advisory panel on Thursday narrowly voted 8-6 to ease restrictions on several injectable peptides, including BPC-157 and TB-500, despite safety concerns from government scientists. The non-binding recommendation could lead the FDA to allow pharmacies to compound these substances, potentially expanding their use in wellness and cosmetic treatments. Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr., a vocal peptide advocate, has signaled plans to overturn existing FDA restrictions.

stories.title: What to Know About Peptides the FDA Is Considering for Use
stories.summary: The agency is considering whether compounding pharmacies should be allowed to produce BPC-157, TB-500 and other previously banned peptides.

stories.title: Google fined €890m by EU for favouring its own apps over rivals
stories.summary: Google has been fined €890 million by the European Union for abusing its market dominance by favouring its own apps and services over rivals. The penalty, the first major action under the EU’s Digital Markets Act, follows two breaches where Google gave its services an unfair advantage. Google warns the ruling could strip away key features for European users, but EU officials insist the measures protect fair competition.

stories.title: Judge Extends Pause for Paramount-Warner Bros. Deal
stories.summary: The court blocked the companies from closing the acquisition through mid-August, as a judge considers two lawsuits challenging it.

stories.title: India activist ends hunger strike after 26 days
stories.summary: India’s prominent activist Sonam Wangchuk ended a 26-day hunger strike after the government guaranteed no legal action against protesters at Delhi’s Jantar Mantar. The government ministers visited him in hospital as mass demonstrations continued following a violent police crackdown on Monday’s march to parliament. Youth-led group CJP, now a major dissent voice, vows to keep protesting until Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan resigns.

stories.title: King's Tardis entrance as Commonwealth Games begin
stories.summary: The Commonwealth Games opening ceremony in Glasgow featured a surprise appearance by King Charles III, who entered via a Tardis-like portal. Scotland’s team drew the loudest cheers as they marched in kilts, followed by a Loch Ness Monster float and a rousing rendition of Flower of Scotland. The event showcased Scottish culture with performances by Nathan Evans and Callum Beattie before 10,000 spectators.

stories.title: Number of U.S. Troop Deaths in Iran War Appears Lower on Pentagon Website
stories.summary: Three military officials and a Pentagon spokesman gave conflicting explanations for why the number of Americans killed in the Iran war had changed.

stories.title: FDA Panel Supports Lifting Restrictions on Four Peptides
stories.summary: In an all-day hearing, the agency’s scientists argued against allowing broad access to unproven drugs that the agency had banned three years ago.

stories.title: Amid Cyclospora Outbreak Tied to Lettuce, America Skips Salads
stories.summary: Foot traffic to chains such as Chopt and Chipotle fell in recent weeks after a recall from Taylor Farms and changing messages from the F.D.A.

stories.title: Health Care Agency Discontinues Dozens of Active Research Awards
stories.summary: The Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality slowed the awarding of new grants last year. Now it is stopping existing ones.

stories.title: Hull KR inflict more derby misery on Hull FC
stories.summary: Hull KR beat Hull FC 28-20 in a tense Super League derby, ending their losing streak with nine straight wins over their rivals. Hull FC, already second-bottom, suffered their sixth straight defeat despite an early 12-0 lead. The Robins moved into fifth in the play-off race.

stories.title: Intel Benefits From a New Shift in A.I. Spending
stories.summary: The Silicon Valley chipmaker’s revenue rose 25 percent in the latest quarter, its fastest growth in 15 years, as A.I. firms increasingly bought chips known as central processing units.

stories.title: Key Republican Senator Says He Will Support Erica Schwartz for C.D.C. Director
stories.summary: Senator Bill Cassidy, who is believed to hold the deciding vote in the Senate health committee for Dr. Erica Schwartz’s confirmation, had declined for days to say whether he would back her.
